text.title: Backlog Prioritization Frameworks
text.summary: RICE scoring, MoSCoW classification, and Value vs Effort matrix — when to apply each.

rule.statement: RICE score = (Reach x Impact x Confidence) / Effort. Always flag agent-generated RICE scores with [estimate] — they are hypotheses until validated with real user data.
rule.rationale: Reach and Impact require real user data the agent does not have. Agents systematically assign 100% Confidence; default to 50-80% for agent-generated scores and require justification for 100%.

example.code: | Feature | Reach | Impact | Confidence | Effort | RICE Score | Notes | |---------|-------|--------|------------|--------|------------|-------| | Auth refactor | 8 [est] | 2 [est] | 80% [est] | 2 | 6.4 | estimate — needs user research |
example.note: Every score tagged [est] so future readers (human and agent) know the data source.

rule.statement: Use RICE for quarterly roadmap planning and data-driven feature comparison. Require at least Reach and Impact to be estimated before scoring.
rule.rationale: RICE without Reach and Impact estimates is arbitrary ranking with extra steps. Skip RICE if user data is not available and use MoSCoW instead.

rule.statement: Must items must not exceed 60% of total release scope. If everything is Must, require the team to demote items until the constraint is met.
rule.rationale: Unrestricted Must lists defeat the purpose of MoSCoW. The 60% constraint forces genuine prioritization.

rule.statement: Document every Won't item with a one-sentence reason and a "when" (Phase 2, v2, never). The Won't-Do section in roadmap.md prevents the same items from being re-proposed in every grooming session.
rule.rationale: Items without explicit exclusion reasoning re-emerge in every planning session, wasting grooming time on decisions already made.

list.item: Quick sprint decisions or resource constraints: MoSCoW
Quarterly roadmap with data available: RICE
Small team, lightweight process: Value vs Effort 2x2 matrix
Strategic theme identification across a large backlog: Outcome-Based (OKR-linked)
